evals.name: promote-feedback-service-tag
evals.prompt: I just merged a feedback-service PR and CI built image tag 8a2bc5d. Promote it to staging. Tell me the exact file to change, the exact YAML edit, the commit message, and any rules I need to follow before pushing.
evals.expected_output: Routes to b81-platform. Names the exact path argocd/deployments/staging/feedback-service/values.yaml. Shows the wave-app.image.tag edit. Conventional Commits message. Mentions GPG-signed commit requirement. Does NOT touch b81-kubernetes.

evals.name: add-new-backend-service
evals.prompt: We're adding a new backend microservice called 'notification-service' to staging. It runs Python, needs a Postgres database, and consumes Pub/Sub. Walk me through what files I need to create and where, and what the values.yaml should look like.
evals.expected_output: Routes to b81-platform. Two-step add: argocd/applications/values.staging.yaml registration + argocd/deployments/staging/notification-service/{Chart.yaml,values.yaml}. Backend-with-worker template (configs:true, includeDatabaseProxy:true). Mentions optional Pulumi step for GCP resources. References b81-platform/docs/adding-new-service.md.

evals.name: bump-wave-app-chart
evals.prompt: I want to add HPA support for workers in the wave-app chart. Where do I make the change, what process do I follow before merging, and do I need to update anything in the platform repo at the same time?
evals.expected_output: Routes to b81-kubernetes/helm/charts/wave-app/. Mentions make bump-chart wave-app, CHANGELOG.md and README.md updates required, make can-i-push. Explicitly says the b81-platform Chart.yaml bump should happen in a SEPARATE PR after the chart is published. Does NOT recommend editing both repos together.

evals.name: list-production-services
evals.prompt: What services are currently running in production?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT recall a service list from memory or guess. Directs the user to read b81-platform/argocd/applications/values.production.yaml as the source of truth, OR reads it directly if the repo is accessible. If recalled, must caveat that the list can drift and tell the user to verify.
